title: cyanococcus pensylvanicus
intro: Cyanococcus pensylvanicus, commonly known as the American cranberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to wetlands in North America. The medicinal properties of the American cranberry lie in its proanthocyanidins, which have been shown to inhibit the adhesion of bacteria to bladder and urinary tract epithelial cells, preventing infections. The health benefits of consuming American cranberry include reducing the risk of urinary tract infections, supporting cardiovascular health, and potentially aiding in the management of certain cancer treatments. American cranberry is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and juices, which can be found in health food stores and online retailers.
